info.title: Linkplay/WiiM HTTP API
info.description:
HTTP API for Linkplay/WiiM devices. This API is based on the HTTP API for WiiM PRODUCTS Version 1.2, with additions from others.

You can send 'HTTPS: Get' requests to the device, most of the responses are a simple 'OK' or in the JSON format.


The request format is 'https://x.x.x.x/httpapi.asp?command=********'

- Where x.x.x.x is the IP address of your WiiM device (In the descriptions, we assume the IP of the device is 10.10.10.254)
- ******* is the actual command.


Commands can contain both integers and strings, denoted as '%d' for integers and '%s' for strings, respectively.


This Swagger doc uses a reverse proxy to access the device. See the description for the actual command.

Note that Linkplay is a provider of the hardware and software for these devices. WiiM is a brand that uses Linkplay hardware and software.
However other brands may also use Linkplay hardware and software, so this API may or may not work with other brands as well, such as Arylic.

If a command doesn't work with your device, it may be that the command is not supported by the device or that the device is not a WiiM device. The command may also be specific to a certain version of the firmware. 

If you have a different brand, you may want to check the documentation for that brand. You'll find commands here that are deprecated: these are commands that are no longer supported by the device or may be brand specific. You can still use them, but they may not work as expected.

servers.description: Reverse proxy to access the device. The actual devices are set using config.yaml. Please see the README for more information.

tags.name: Generic
tags.description:
Generic command for testing  
This not an actual endpoint, but a means to test for commands not explicitly defined in the HTTP API.

tags.name: Presets
tags.description:
Manage presets

The WiiM Home App allows users to configure 12 presets for quick access to
preferred radio stations, playlists, mixes, albums, or artists. Each preset is accessible through its assigned number.

tags.name: Audio output control
tags.description: Get and set audio output mode

tags.name: Room correction
tags.description: Get and set room correction settings

tags.name: Amazon music
tags.description: Get and set Amazon Music settings

tags.name: Other
tags.description: Existing commands, purpose unkown. To research further...

get.description:
Generic command to the device. All calls will follow the format /httpapi.asp?command={command}.


The actual command is passed as a path parameter. With this, you can test out any command that is not explicitly defined in this API.

The response could be a simple 'OK' or an object with data. The actual response will depend on the command sent. If you get an 'unknown command' response, the command is not supported by the device.


Examples:
-  'getStatusEx' will do an /httpapi.asp?command=getStatusEx
-  'setPlayerCmd:pause' will do an /httpapi.asp?command=setPlayerCmd:pause
-  'setPlayerCmd:resume' will do an /httpapi.asp?command=setPlayerCmd:resume

externalDocs.description: HTTP API for WiiM PRODUCTS Version 1.2

parameters.name: command
parameters.description: The actual command to send to the device. Note that the command is case-sensitive.

get.description:
Makes a call to https://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=getStatusEx

Retrieves detailed informations about a connected device.

The documented output may or may not be complete or differ from what the device actually reports.

externalDocs.description: HTTP API for WiiM PRODUCTS Version 1.2

get.description:
Makes a call to https://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=getDebugInfo

Retrieves debug information about a connected device.

The documented output may or may not be complete or differ from what the device actually reports.

JSON Response
{
 "metaData": {
  "album": "Country Heat",
  "title": "Old Dirt Roads",
  "subtitle": "",
  "artist": "Owen Riegling",
  "albumArtURI ": "https://m.media-amazon.com/images/I/51iU0odzJwL.jpg",
  "sampleRate ": "44100",
  "bitDepth": "16",
  "bitRate": "63",
  "trackId": "s6707",
 }
}

get.summary: Play a specific track from a playlist by URL and index
get.description:
http://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=setPlayerCmd:hex_playlist:url:<index>

Play the URl (URI is the m3u or ASX playlist link, index is the start
index), here, url should be hexed (please refer to 1.3)

get.description:
https://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=setPlayerCmd:play:url

Response is always 'OK' now

get.description:
https://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=setPlayerCmd:playlist:url:<index>

Play the playlist with the URL (URL points to the m3u or ASX playlist
link, index is the start index).

Response is always 'OK' now.

get.description:
https://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=setPlayerCmd:loopmode:n

n = 
- -1: ...
- 0: Shuffle disabled, Repeat enabled - loop
- 1: Shuffle disabled, Repeat enabled - loop once
- 2: Shuffle enabled, Repeat enabled - loop
- 3: Shuffle enabled, Repeat disabled
- 4: Shuffle disabled, Repeat disabled
- 5: Shuffle enabled, Repeat enabled - loop once

externalDocs.description: HTTP API for WiiM PRODUCTS Version 1.2

parameters.name: n
parameters.description:
- -1: ...
- 0: Shuffle disabled, Repeat enabled - loop
- 1: Shuffle disabled, Repeat enabled - loop once
- 2: Shuffle enabled, Repeat enabled - loop
- 3: Shuffle enabled, Repeat disabled
- 4: Shuffle disabled, Repeat disabled
- 5: Shuffle enabled, Repeat enabled - loop once

get.description:
Makes a call to https://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=getStaticIpInfo

If the WiiM device is configured to use DHCP, the above string values will be empty (see the ethernet related keys).

get.description: Query current networking status

Makes a call to
https://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=wlanGetConnectState


Note the return result is not in json.


Return string:

Return string Description

PROCESS In progress

PAIRFAIL Wrong password

FAIL Connect fail

OK connected

get.description:
Example: https://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=setWlanStaticIp:ipAddr=192.168.1.100/24:gateWay=192.168.1.1:dnsServer=8.8.8.8

Keyword args:
- ipAddr: Static IP address to set + netmask
- gateWay: Gateway
- dnsServer: The DNS Server (default: 8.8.8.8)

get.summary: Change the SSID name of the device
get.description:
Example: http://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=setSSID:value

Sets a new network name (SSID) of the device in hexadecimal format

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The new SSID name to set for the device, in hexadecimal format.

get.description:
Example: http://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=setNetwork:1:password

Setting a new password using letters and / or numbers and crossing of setnetwork setting: 1 (Securing WIFI network (WPAPSK) in contrast, setnetwork: 0 (OPEN) makes the network open WIFI.

No response The system restarts after the validation of the new password.

parameters.name: n
parameters.description: Network security type, where 1 secures the WIFI network with WPAPSK and 0 makes the network open.

parameters.name: password
parameters.description: The new password to set for the WIFI network, using letters and/or numbers.

externalDocs.description: HTTP API for WiiM PRODUCTS Version 1.2

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name in the list returned by EQGetList, i.e., EQLoad:Flat

get.summary: Set (a specific) band of the 10-band EQ
get.description:
https://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=EQSetBand:{"EQBand":[{"index":0,"param_name":"band31hz","value":10}]}

A way to control the 10-band EQ, including real-time updates from external inputs.

Example 1 - single band change to 10:

https://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=EQSetBand:{"EQBand":[{"index":0,"param_name":"band31hz","value":10}]}'

Example 2 - sets all bands to 52:

https://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=EQSetBand:{"EQBand":[{"index":0,"param_name":"band31hz","value":52},{"index":1,"param_name":"band63hz","value":52},{"index":2,"param_name":"band125hz","value":52},{"index":3,"param_name":"band250hz","value":52},{"index":4,"param_name":"band500hz","value":52},{"index":5,"param_name":"band1khz","value":52},{"index":6,"param_name":"band2khz","value":52},{"index":7,"param_name":"band4khz","value":52},{"index":8,"param_name":"band8khz","value":52},{"index":9,"param_name":"band16khz","value":52}]}'


Notes:
- using WiiM Ultra with firmware version 5.2.809757 (beta), build date 20260304
- works instantly on the device
- changes the currently selected source's EQ
- values are 0-99 (50 = flat)
- app UI reflects changes after leaving/re-entering the EQ screen for that source
- driven from a wireless EQ deck that posts physical slider positions to MQTT, which are then read, scaled, and sent on to the WiiM
- with ~0.4s throttle the WiiM keeps up and tracks slider movements well
- EQGetBand tells you info about your current source, including the band values, which is how I worked this out.

externalDocs.description: Found by 'steve prior' at 'Working EQ API (real-time control)'

parameters.name: n1
parameters.description: Index of the EQ band to set, where 0 corresponds to the 31Hz band, 1 to the 62Hz band, and so on up to 9 for the 16kHz band.

parameters.name: str
parameters.description: Name of the parameter to set, which should be in the format "band{frequency}hz", where {frequency} is the center frequency of the band (e.g., "band31hz", "band63hz", "band125hz", "band250hz", "band500hz", "band1khz", "band2khz", "band4khz", "band8khz", "band16khz").

parameters.name: n2
parameters.description: Value to set for the specified EQ band, where 0-99 represents the gain level (50 = flat, values above 50 boost the band, and values below 50 cut the band).

get.summary: Get LV2 source band with extra parameters
Get LV2 band with extra parameters

externalDocs.description: WiiM Extended HTTP API by DanBrezeanu

get.description:
If the device has no internet access, you need to sync its time with:


http://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=timeSync:YYYYMMDDHHMMSS


YYYY is year (such as 2015), MM is month (01~12), DD is day (01~31), HH
is hour (00~23),

MM is minute (00~59), SS is second (00~59)


In UTC

externalDocs.description: HTTP API for WiiM PRODUCTS Version 1.2

get.description:
http://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=setAlarmClock:n:trig:op:time[:day][:url]


n: 0~2, currently support max 3 alarm


trig: the alarm trigger:

0 cancel the alarm, for example: setAlarmClock:n:0

1 once, day should be YYYYMMDD

2 every day

3 every week, day should be 2 bytes ("00"~"06"), means from Sunday to
Saturday.

4 every week, day should be 2 bytes, the bit 0 to bit 6 means the
effect, for example,

"7F" means every day in week, "01" means only Sunday

5 every month, day should be 2 bytes ("01"~"31")


op: the action

0 shell execute

1 playback or ring

2 stop playback

time: should be HHMMSS, in UTC


day:

if trigger is 0 or 2, no need to set.

if trigger is 1, should be YYYYMMDD ( %04d%02d%02d)

if trigger is 3, day should be 2 bytes ("00"~"06"), means from Sunday to
Saturday.

if trigger is 4, day should be 2 bytes, the bit 0 to bit 6 means the
effect, for example,

"7F" means every day in week, "01" means only Sunday

if trigger is 5, day should be 2 bytes ("01"~"31")


url: the shell path or playback url, should less than 256 bytes

externalDocs.description: HTTP API for WiiM PRODUCTS Version 1.2

parameters.name: n
parameters.description:
The alarm clock number

currently support max 3 alarm

parameters.name: trig
parameters.description:
The alarm trigger


0 cancel the alarm, for example: setAlarmClock:n:0

1 once, day should be YYYYMMDD

2 every day

3 every week, day should be 2 bytes (00"~"06"), means from Sunday to
Saturday.

4 every week, day should be 2 bytes, the bit 0 to bit 6 means the
effect, for example,

"7F" means every day in week, "01" means only Sunday

5 every month, day should be 2 bytes ("01"~"31")

parameters.name: op
parameters.description:
The action

0 shell execute
1 playback or ring
2 stop playback

parameters.name: day
parameters.description:
if trigger is 0 or 2, no need to set.


if trigger is 1, should be YYYYMMDD ( %04d%02d%02d)


if trigger is 3, day should be 2 bytes ("00"~"06"), means from
Sunday to Saturday.


if trigger is 4, day should be 2 bytes, the bit 0 to bit 6 means the
effect, for example,

"7F" means every day in week, "01" means only Sunday


if trigger is 5, day should be 2 bytes ("01"~"31")

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: The shell path or playback url, should less than 256 bytes

get.description:
http://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=getAlarmClock:n

n: 0~2, currently support max 3 alarm
{"enable":"1",
"trigger":"%d",
"operation":"%d",
"date"::"%02d:%02d:%02d", //if not a "every day" alarm, no this
"week_day":"%d", //if not a "every week" alarm, no this
"day":"%02d", //if not a "every month" alarm, no this
"time":"%02d:02d:%02d",
"path":"%s""}

get.description:
http://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=getPresetInfo


JSON Response

{
  "preset_num": 3,
  "preset_list": [
  {
    "number": 1,
    "name": "BBC Radio Norfolk",
    "url": "http:\/\/as-hls-wwlive.akamaized.net\/pool_904\/live\/ww\/bbc_radio_norfolk\/bbc_radio_norfolk.isml\/bbc_radio_norfolk-audio%3d96000.norewind.m3u8",
    "source": "Linkplay Radio",
    "picurl": "http:\/\/cdnprofiles.tunein.com\/s6852\/images\/logoq.jpg?t=638353933090000000"
  }, 
  {
    "number": 6,
    "name": "Radio Paradise",
    "url": "http:\/\/stream.radioparadise.com\/flacm",
    "source": "RadioParadise",
    "picurl": "https:\/\/cdnprofiles.tunein.com\/s13606\/images\/logod.png?t=637541039930000000"
  }, 
  {
    "number": 8,
    "name": "1. Country Heat",
    "url": "unknow",
    "source": "Prime",
    "picurl": "https:\/\/m.media-amazon.com\/images\/I\/51uR6AJUAQL.jpg"
  }
  ]
}


Description

Field type Description

preset_num int Total number of presets

preset_list json array Preset list information

number int Preset index (Start from 1)

name string Playlist name

url string Play url

source string Music source

picurl string Cover picture url

2: AUDIO_OUTPUT_AUX_MODE

0: disable
1: active
audiocast Audio cast output mode:

1: active

get.summary: Get SPDIF sample rate switch latency
get.description:
Example: https://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=getSpdifOutSwitchDelayMs

Observations:
- This value only makes sense if the output interface is SPDIF (optical out).
- Value can be retrieved even if output is not SPDIF

externalDocs.description: WiiM Extended HTTP API by DanBrezeanu

get.summary: Set SPDIF sample rate switch latency
get.description:
Example: https://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=setSpdifOutSwitchDelayMs:800

Observations:
- Delay is in milliseconds
- Value can be set even if output is not SPDIF
- Delay is upper bound to 3000 milliseconds

externalDocs.description: WiiM Extended HTTP API by DanBrezeanu

Example: https://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=getChannelBalance

get.description:
Example: https://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=setChannelBalance:0

parameters.description: Not sure what this argument means, I've only seen the app use the value 3 for it. Works with other integers and successfully starts a BT scan. Maybe a max list size?

get.description:
First do a startbtdiscovery:3: to start the scan, then use this to get the result.

Output (JSON):
num: Number of found devices
scan_status: BT scan step
  0: Not started
  1: Initializing
  2: ??? (never seen it)
  3: Scanning
  4: Finished scanning
list: List of found devices

Already paired devices may be listed as well.

externalDocs.description: WiiM Extended HTTP API by DanBrezeanu

get.description: Clears the Bluetooth device scan results

scan_status: BT scan step
1: Initializing
2: ??? (never seen it)
3: Scanning
4: Finished scanning
list: List of found devices

get.description:
Example: https://192.168.1.100/httpapi.asp?command=connectbta2dpsynk:9c:2a:be:2e:ce:f1

Output:
Success: OK
Error: Failed

Observations: The device should be an Audio Sink (e.g. speaker, A/V reciever). The audio output will be changed to the device.

get.summary: Connect to a Bluetooth source device
Start getting Bluetooth pair device status
Pair with a BLE HID device
Remove all paired BLE HID devices
Get the current room correction settings
get.description:
Makes a call to https://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=RoomCorrGet

It returns a JSON object with the current room correction settings, including the EQ levels for each band, the source name, and other related information.  
The "EQBand" array contains objects for each EQ band, with parameters such as "mode", "freq", "q", and "gain".  
This information can be used to understand the current room correction configuration and make informed adjustments if needed.

status.description: Status of the response, typically "OK" if the request was successful.

source_name.description: The name of the current audio source.

EQStat.description: The status of the EQ, such as "On" or "Off".

Name.description: The name of the current room correction profile.

pluginURI.description: The URI of the plugin used for room correction.

index.description: The index of the EQ band.

param_name.description: The name of the parameter for this band (e.g., "a_mode", "a_freq", "a_q", "a_gain").

value.description: The value of the parameter for this band.

get.summary: Get the current room correction mode
get.description:
Makes a call to https://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=RoomCorrGetMode

Same as RoomCorrGet?
Makes a call to https://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=RoomCorrSet:{str}

get.summary: Set the room correction settings for left and right channels
get.description:
Makes a call to https://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=RoomCorrSetLR:{str}

Same as RoomCorrSet?

get.summary: Set the room correction settings with version and time parameters

Makes a call to https://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=getAlexaProfile
Makes a call to https://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=alexaLogOut
Makes a call to https://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=getAlexaCountry
Makes a call to https://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=alexaLanguageListGet
Makes a call to https://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=alexaGetLanguage
Makes a call to https://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=setAlexaCountry:{str}

parameters.name: str
parameters.description: Country code to set for Alexa

get.description: Makes a call to https://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=alexaSetLanguage:{str}

parameters.name: str
parameters.description: Language code to set for Alexa

get.description: Makes a call to https://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=setAmazonAccessToken:{str}:{str2}
Makes a call to https://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=EnableAmazonAtmos:%d

parameters.name: n
parameters.description: 1 to enable, 0 to disable Amazon Atmos

get.description: Makes a call to https://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=EnableAmazonHD:%d

parameters.name: n
parameters.description: 1 to enable, 0 to disable Amazon HD

get.description: Makes a call to https://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=GetAmazonHD
Makes a call to https://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=getAmazonConfig

Makes a call to https://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=setAmazonMusicParams:code=%@:redirect_uri=%@:client_id=%@:code_verifier=%@
Note: The parameters in the command should be replaced with actual values when making the request.

parameters.name: str1
parameters.description: The code parameter for Amazon Music

parameters.name: str2
parameters.description: The redirect URI for Amazon Music

parameters.name: str3
parameters.description: The client ID for Amazon Music

parameters.name: str4
parameters.description: The code verifier for Amazon Music

get.summary: Get a list of LinkPlay available
get.description:
Example: http://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=multiroom:getSlaveList

Allows to obtain the list of the devices using the same communication protocol.

JSON output:
{
  "Slaves": "1",
  "Slave_list": [
    {
      "Name": "FA5100_a3f4",
      "Mask": "0",
      "Volume": "90",
      "Mute": "0",
      "Channel": "0",
      "Ip": "10.10.10.100",
      "Version": "WIFIAudio.1.2.2321"
    }
  ]
}

Field	Description
Slaves	Number of LinkPlay available
Slave_list	Information about each Sonoé iEast case available
name	Name
mask	Case already in Multi-Room mode, Yes = 1, No = 0
Volume	Volume level
Mute	Enable Mute mode, Yes = 1, Off = 0
Channel	Wifi channel
ip	IP address of the LinkPlay Boot
version	firmware version

get.summary: Removing a LinkPlay from the multi-room
get.description:
Example: http://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=multiroom:SlaveKickout:ip

Allows to remove from the multiroom mode a device Based on its IP address.

parameters.name: ip
parameters.description: The IP address of the LinkPlay device to be removed from multi-room mode.

get.summary: Hide the IP address of a LinkPlay
get.description:
Example: http://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=multiroom:SlaveMask:ip

Allows to integrate a multi-room mode a device Based on its IP address. It is thus rendered invisible from the IP network.

parameters.name: ip
parameters.description: The IP address of the LinkPlay device to be hidden from the IP network in multi-room mode.

get.description:
Example: http://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=multiroom:SlaveUnMask:ip

Allows to release from the multi-room mode based on its IP address. It is thus visible on the IP network.

parameters.name: ip
parameters.description: The IP address of the LinkPlay device to be released from multi-room mode.

get.description:
Example: http://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=multiroom:SlaveVolume:ip:volume

Allows you to adjust the individual volume of the each of the devicees via the IP address of the device and the definition of a value going from from 1 to 100. Note: these settings will be lost when deactivating multi-room mode (kickout)

parameters.name: ip
parameters.description: The IP address of the LinkPlay device to adjust the volume for in multi-room mode.

parameters.name: volume
parameters.description: The volume level to set for the specified LinkPlay device, ranging from 1 to 100.

get.description:
Example: http://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=setPlayerCmd:slave_vol:volume

Adjusts the overal volume of the multi-room with the definition of a value ranging from 1 to 100. Note: thesis settings will be lost When disabling multi-room mode

parameters.name: volume
parameters.description: The overall volume level to set for multi-room mode, ranging from 1 to 100.

get.description:
Example: http://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=multiroom:SlaveMute:ip:mute

Enables the Mute on each device via the IP address of the device and by enabling (1) and disabling (0)

parameters.name: ip
parameters.description: The IP address of the LinkPlay device to adjust the mute status for in multi-room mode.

parameters.name: mute
parameters.description: Mute status to set for the specified LinkPlay device, where 1 enables mute and 0 disables mute.

get.description:
Example: http://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=setPlayerCmd:slave_mute:mute

Enables the Mute on all devices

Example: http://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=setPlayerCmd:slave_mute:unmute

To disable the mute mode overall the

get.summary: Individual management of the audio signal Right / left
get.description:
Example: http://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=multiroom:SlaveChannel:ip:channel

Enables the Mute on Each of the devices via the IP address of the device and by this parameter to signal right only Channe0 = left and only 1 = Channel signal

parameters.name: ip
parameters.description: The IP address of the LinkPlay device to adjust the audio channel for in multi-room mode.

parameters.name: channel
parameters.description: Audio channel to set for the specified LinkPlay device, where 0 signals right only and 1 signals left only.

get.summary: Overal management of the audio signal Right / left
get.description:
Example: http://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=setPlayerCmd:slave_channel:channel

Enables the Mute on Each of devices via the IP address of the device and by this parameter to signal right only Channel= 0 and left signal only 1 = Channel

parameters.name: channel
parameters.description: Audio channel to set for all devices in multi-room mode, where 0 signals right only and 1 signals left only.

get.summary: Individual definition of the device Name
get.description:
Example: http://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=multiroom:SlaveSetDeviceName:%ip:%s

Individual settings of the name of the square in mode UPnP / Airplay

parameters.name: ip
parameters.description: The IP address of the LinkPlay device to set the device name for in multi-room mode.

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: The device name to set for the specified LinkPlay device in multi-room mode.

get.description:
Example: http://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=multiroom:Ungroup

Disables the multi-room mode

Example: http://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=multiroom:ConfigGet:realtime_cache_limit

Get the real-time cache limit in multi-room mode. The value is in seconds.

Example: http://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=multiroom:ConfigSet:realtime_cache_limit:%d

Set the real-time cache limit in multi-room mode. The value is in seconds.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The real-time cache limit to set for multi-room mode, in seconds.

get.summary: Start WPS on a LinkPlay device
get.description:
Example: http://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=multiroom:SlaveStartWPS:%s

Start WPS on a LinkPlay device based on its IP address. This can be used to connect the device to a new Wi-Fi network.

parameters.name: ip
parameters.description: The IP address of the LinkPlay device to start WPS on.

get.summary: Get the list of group names in multi-room mode
get.description:
Example: http://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=multiroom:getnamegrouplist

Get the list of group names in multi-room mode. The output is a JSON array of strings, where each string is a group name.

Response example:
{ "grouplist": [ ], "current_group_id": "" }

grouplist.description: List of group names in multi-room mode

current_group_id.description: The ID of the currently selected group

parameters.name: IP
parameters.description: The IP address of the multi-room group to join.

parameters.name: uuid
parameters.description: The UUID of the multi-room group to join.

parameters.name: ip
parameters.description: The IP address of the slave device

externalDocs.description: WiiM Forums - API for Wiim Amp?

get.description:
Example: http://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=getMvRemoteUpdateStartCheck

Search for the firmware updates available

Example: http://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=getMvRemoteUpdateStart

After calling the interface, if a new version is available, the device starts to download updates. At the end of the download, the update process starts.
WARNING: DO NOT POWER OFF DURING THIS PROCESS!!

Example: http://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=getMvRemoteUpdateStatus

Get the download progress.

Value	Meaning
- 10	Under review
- 20  ...
- 21	The verification of the downloaded update file failed
- 22	Downloading the update file failed
- 23	The verification of the downloaded update file failed
- 25	Start downloading
- 27	Download complete
- 30	Downloading and verification completed

Example: http://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=getMvRomBurnPrecent

Status of updating

{
  " status ":" 0 "," progress ":" 50 "
}

Fields	Meaning
status	State
progress	Progress between 0 to 100%

Example: http://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=restoreToDefault

To restore factory settings, erasing defined configurations. The device restarts.

Example: http://10.10.10.254/httpapi.asp?command=setDeviceName:%s

Sets the name UPnP, DLNA and Airplay of the device (Hex)

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The new device name to set for UPnP, DLNA, and Airplay, in hexadecimal format.

ssid.description:
Name of the device. Device's own SSID when in WiFi pairing
mode or when device's WiFi hotspot is active

hideSSID.description:
When the device is operating as a WiFi hotspot, this flag
determines whether its SSID should be hidden or visible 0:
ssid is visible 1: ssid is hidden

build.description:
Possible values: release, debug, backup release: this is a
release version debug: this is a debug version backup:
this is a backup version

priv_prj.description: Project name which would stand for a specific board

cast_enable.description:
Flag for casting feature 0: casting is disabled 1: casting
is enabled

group.description:
0 means it's a master speaker or standalone,  1 means a
slave speaker in group

master_uuid.description:
Exist when working in slave mode, showing the UUID of
master device.

slave_mask.description:
Exist when working in slave mode, showing if the device
support mask feature. 0 means not supported.

wmrm_version.description:
LinkPlay's MRM SDK version, Multiroom library version,
version 4.2 or above won't work with any version below 4.2

internet.description: Current status of internet access: 0: not ready 1: ready

uuid.description:
The unique ID of the device (will remain after device
reboot)

MAC.description:
MAC address of the device when working in hotspot mode,
will show on APP and also the sticker on module/device.

STA_MAC.description: MAC address of the device when working in station mode

BT_MAC.description: The BT MAC address of the device

AP_MAC.description: The MAC address of the AP that the device is connected to

ETH_MAC.description:
The MAC address of the device when it's connected to
ethernet

InitialConfiguration.description:
Flag for initial configuration 0: not configured 1:
configured

netstat.description:
Current WiFi status: 0: no network 1: connected to the
internet 2: connected to the local network

essid.description: SSID of the WiFi the device is connected to in HEX format

apcli0.description: The IP v4 address of the device over WiFi

eth0.description: Device's IP address when it's connected to ethernet

eth2.description: Device's IP address when it's connected to ethernet

eth_dhcp.description:
Flag for DHCP or Static IP Address 0: Static IP 1: IP
Address provided by LAN/WLAN DHCP Server

VersionUpdate.description:
Flag that determines, if there is a new firmware version
available or not. 0: no new firmware 1: new firmware
available

NewVer.description:
If there is a new firmware available (in case of
VersionUpdate is set to 1), this is the new version number

mcu_ver.description: Version of MCU on base board

mcu_ver_new.description:
New version of MCU on base board, indicates if there is a
newer version of MCU available 0 - No new version others -
New version

ra0.description: WiFi AP IP address, normally it is 10.10.10.254

temp_uuid.description: Temporary UUID (will change after device reboot)

cap1.description: Bit mask for the module feature, used internally

capability.description: Bit mask for the module feature, used internally

prompt_status.description:
Indicates if the prompting voice would be played or not,
you can set with command PromptEnable and PromptDisable. 0
- No prompting voice 1 - Have prompting voice

dsp_ver.description: Version for voice processing, not used

dsp_ver_new.description: New version for voice processing, not used

streams.description:
This is a bit mask: 0: If Airplay is enabled 1: If DLNA is
enabled 2: Has TTPod support 3: Has TuneIn support 4: Has
Pandora support 5: Has DoubanFM support

spotify_active.description:
Spotify status 0: Spotify is not playing 1: Spotify is
playing

plm_support.description:
This is a bit mask, each bit stands for an external input
source: bit1: LineIn (Aux support) bit2: Bluetooth support
bit3: USB support bit4: Optical support bit6: Coaxial
support bit8: LineIn 2 support bit15: USBDAC support
Others are reserved or not used.

RSSI.description:
RSSI Level of used WiFi Value ranges from 0 - 100. 100
means best signal strength.

BSSID.description:
The Basic Service Set Identifiers:  In most cases this
will be the MAC Address of the Wireless Acces Point Used
(e.g. Router)

wlanSnr.description: Signal to Noise Ratio of the WiFi connection

wlanNoise.description: Noise level of the WiFi connection

battery.description:
Battery status 0: battery is not charging 1: battery is
charging

battery_percent.description: Battery charge level Value ranges from 0 - 100

auth.description: Type of WiFi Protected Access used (Authentication Key).

encry.description: Type of encryption used for the WiFi connection.

uart_pass_port.description: Port used for TCP/IP Communcations/Socket Connections

udisk.description: USB disk status 0: no USB disk 1: USB disk is connected

DeviceName.description: The device UPnP and Airplay friendly name

GroupName.description: The group name of the device that it belongs to

system_ready.description: Indicates if the system is ready

slave_status.description: Indicates if the device is a slave in multiroom mode

slave_playing.description: Indicates if the slave device is currently playing audio in multiroom mode

slave_num.description: Number of slave devices in multiroom mode

slave_latency.description: Latency for slave devices in multiroom mode

slave_default_latency.description: Default latency for slave devices in multiroom mode

connecting_type.description: Indicates the type of connection for the device

play_mode.description: Current play mode of the device

plm_notify_flag.description: Indicates if there is a PLM notification

play_status.description: Current play status of the device

time_sync_ready.description: Indicates if time synchronization is ready

asr_pause_request.description: Indicates if there is a request to pause ASR

alexa_response_status.description: Status of the response from Alexa

pause_flag_for_alexa.description: Indicates if there is a pause flag for Alexa

priv_languages.description: Supported languages for private use, bit mask

retail_mode.description: Indicates if the device is in retail mode

a01controller_crash.description: Indicates if there has been a crash in the A01 controller

asr_tts_crash.description: Indicates if there has been a crash in ASR or TTS

mcu_crash.description: Indicates if there has been a crash in the MCU

process_deadlock.description: Indicates if there has been a deadlock in any process

gapless.description: Indicates if gapless playback is enabled

slave.description: Indicates if the device is a slave in multiroom mode

wifi_abort_date.description: Date of the last WiFi abort

smplay_skip_silence.description: Indicates if skip silence is enabled for SMPlay

type.description:
0: Main or standalone device 
1: Device is a Multiroom Guest

mode.description:
0: None 
1: AirPlay or AirPlay 2 
2: DLNA streaming 
10: Playing network content, e.g. vTuner, Home Media Share, Amazon Music, Deezer, etc 
11: playing UDISK (Local USB disk on device) 16: TF card playlist 20: playback start by HTTPAPI 
31: Spotify Connect 
32: TIDAL Connect 
40: AUX-In/Line-In input mode 
41: Bluetooth input mode 
42: External storage 
43: Optical-In input mode 
47: Line-In #2 input mode 
50: Mirror 
51: USBDAC input mode 
60: Voice mail
99: The Device is a Guest in a Multiroom Zone

loop.description:
Is a Combination of SHUFFLE and REPEAT modes 0: SHUFFLE:
disabled REPEAT: enabled - loop 1: SHUFFLE: disabled
REPEAT: enabled - loop once 2: SHUFFLE: enabled REPEAT:
enabled - loop 3: SHUFFLE: enabled REPEAT: disabled 4:
SHUFFLE: disabled REPEAT: disabled 5: SHUFFLE: enabled
REPEAT: enabled - loop once

eq.description: The preset number of the Equalizer

status.description:
Playback status stop: no audio selected play: playing
audio load/loading: loading/buffering audio pause: audio
paused

totlen.description: Track duration in ms. Zero when playing a live stream.

Title.description: hexed string of the track title

plicount.description: The total number of tracks in the playlist

vol.description:
Current volume Value range is from 0 - 100. So can be
considered a linear percentage (0% to 100%) of the volume.

mute.description: Current mute state 0: Not muted 1: Muted

wlanStaticIp.description: Wifi IP address of the device

wlanGateWay.description: Wifi IP address of the gateway

wlanDnsServer.description: Wifi IP address of the DNS server

wlanStaticIpEnable.description:
Status of the WiFi static IP configuration
0: Disabled
1: Enabled

ethStaticIp.description: Wired IP address of the device

ethGateWay.description: Wired IP address of the gateway

ethDnsServer.description: Wired IP address of the DNS server

ethStaticIpEnable.description:
Status of the wired static IP configuration
0: Disabled
1: Enabled

scan_status.description:
BT scan step
0: Not started
1: Initializing
2: ??? (never seen it)
3: Scanning
4: Finished scanning

BT_MAC_ADDRESS.name: BT_MAC_ADDRESS
BT_MAC_ADDRESS.description: The Bluetooth MAC address of the device you want to connect to or disconnect from
